title: Umami Irish Boil Hash
description: A hearty, umami-rich Irish dish featuring black olives, boiled potatoes, and a crunchy, pan-seared finish. Can be easily adapted to a shojin-friendly diet by substituting the Irish sausage.

ingredients.name: Smoked Irish sausage
ingredients.notes: optional, or sub with tofu for a shojin-friendly option

instructions: Boil the potatoes, carrots, and onion in salted water until they are tender. Drain and set aside.
Slice the cooked potatoes, carrots, and onion into 1-inch pieces. If using smoked Irish sausage, slice it into 1-inch pieces as well.
Heat the iron skillet or teppan pan over medium-high heat. Add 2 tablespoons of oil and swirl it around to coat the pan.
Add the sliced potatoes, carrots, onion, and sausage to the pan. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes or until the potatoes are golden brown.
Add the black olives, garlic, and bay leaves to the pan. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the hash to a serving platter or individual plates. Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
For a shojin-friendly option, omit the sausage and increase the amount of garlic and bay leaves for added depth of flavor.
